heading: Exceptions, waiver, and termination
label: 9530 Exceptions, waiver, and termination

children.text: Activities subject to the reporting requirements under title V of the National Security Act of 1947 ( et seq.), or any authorized intelligence activities of the United States.50 U.S.C. 3091

The admission of an alien to the United States if such admission is necessary to comply with United States obligations under the Agreement between the United Nations and the United States of America regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , under the Convention on Consular Relations, done at Vienna , and entered into force , or under other international agreements.June 26, 1947November 21, 1947April 24, 1963March 19, 1967

children.heading: Waiver of sanctions that are imposed
children.label: b Waiver of sanctions that are imposed
children.text: Subject to , if the President imposes sanctions with respect to a person under this part or the amendments made by this part, the President may waive the application of those sanctions if the President determines that such a waiver is in the national security interest of the United States.section 9511 of this title
a notice of and justification for the termination; and

children.label: A
children.text: the person is not engaging in the activity that was the basis for the sanctions or has taken significant verifiable steps toward stopping the activity; and

children.label: B
children.text: the President has received reliable assurances that the person will not knowingly engage in activity subject to sanctions under this part in the future.
